data.title: Positive News for ARB Corp in Vehicle Sales — Market Talk
data.description: An improvement in sales of the top 11 models fitted with ARB Corp. equipment is linked almost entirely to a single vehicle, Canaccord Genuity analysts point out. They tell clients in a note that while the 6.3% on-year rise is positive for the Australian accessory manufacturer, it mostly relates to an unusual jump in sales of the Toyota Prado. This stems from strong current sales of a new model comparing with low year-earlier sales as buyers waited on its release, they observe. However, they add that the Prado is a high-fitment rate vehicle, so the surge will be helpful to ARB. Canaccord Genuity has a hold rating and A$34.70 target price on the stock, which is down 0.3% at A$31.77. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Changan Automobile to Spin Off As Independent Enterprise In Revamp
data.description:
Chongqing Changan Automobile will be spun off from its indirect controlling shareholder, China South Industries Group, according to a Shenzhen bourse filing on Thursday.

The State-owned Assets Supervision and Administration Commission of the State Council approved that the Chinese automobile maker will be separated into an independent central enterprise as China South Industries undergoes a corporate split.

The equity of the remaining China South Industries will be contributed as capital to China North Industries Group.

Changan said the revamp will not have a significant impact on its operations and production, the filing said.

data.title: Singapore Dollar Steady Ahead of U.S. Nonfarm Payrolls Report — Market Talk
data.description: The Singapore dollar is steady against its U.S. counterpart in the Asian trading session. Weak U.S. economic data weighs on the greenback, MUFG Bank's Lloyd Chan says in a research report. If the U.S. nonfarm payrolls report comes in much weaker than expected, this could spark a dovish repricing of the Fed's rate trajectory for 2H, the senior currency analyst says. This would probably weigh on the U.S. dollar and support capital inflows into Asia, Chan adds. USD/SGD is little changed at 1.2862. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Sinopec Tianjin brings No.3 PP unit on stream
data.description: Sinopec Tianjin Co has restarted its No.3 Polypropylene (PP) unit.A Polymerupdate source in China informed a team member,

data.title: HSBC Issues $2 Billion Perpetual Convertible Securities
data.description:
HSBC issued $2 billion of 7.050% perpetual subordinated contingent convertible securities on Thursday.

The securities will be traded on Euronext Dublin's Global Exchange Market, the British lender said in a same-day release.

data.title: Sunshine Guojian Pharmaceutical Advances Gout Drug Toward Approval in China
data.description:
Sunshine Guojian Pharmaceutical Shanghai said its anti-IL-1β monoclonal antibody (SSGJ-613) for acute gouty arthritis secured new drug application acceptance from China's drug regulator.

Phase three trial results showed the therapy demonstrated pain relief on par with steroids and superior recurrence prevention, with a favorable safety profile, according to a Friday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

data.title: Hunan Zhongke Electric to Build Oman Battery Plant for 8 Billion Yuan, Postpones Morocco Project
data.description:
Hunan Zhongke Electric plans to invest 8 billion yuan to construct a production base in Oman, according to a Shenzhen bourse disclosure on Wednesday.

The plant will produce 200,000 tons of lithium-ion battery anode materials every year.

The project will be constructed in two phases, each with a capacity of 100,000 tons per year, with an expected duration of 36 months for each phase.

Meanwhile, the Chinese battery producer postponed its project in Morocco to ensure focused and orderly overseas expansion, the disclosure said.

data.title: Iron ore heads for weekly gains on resilient China demand
data.description:
Iron ore futures prices rose to one-week high on Friday and were set for weekly gains, buoyed by progress in Sino-U.S. talks and steady demand from top consumer China, although seasonally slow steel consumption capped gains.

The most-traded September iron ore contract on China's Dalian Commodity Exchange (DCE) was up 1.21% at 710 yuan ($98.84) a metric ton, as of 0231 GMT, the highest since May 29.

The contract has logged gains of 0.9% so far this week.

The benchmark July iron ore (SZZFN5) on the Singapore Exchange was up 0.9% at $95.7 a ton, as of 0226 GMT, rising 0.1% this week.

It touched its highest since May 29 at $96.4 earlier in the session.

Market sentiment was upbeat after U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping confronted weeks of brewing trade tensions and a battle over critical minerals in a rare leader-to-leader call on Thursday.

"The call between the Sino-U.S. leaders is a sign of easing trade tensions between the two superpowers, sparking risk-on sentiment," analysts at Everbright Futures said in a note.

Near-term ore demand is also expected to stay firm as steelmakers need to stockpile cargo to sustain production, with hot metal output hovering at relatively high levels and mills' in-plant inventory staying low, analysts at Chaos Ternary Futures said.

Average daily hot metal output, a gauge of iron ore demand, stood at 2.42 million tons as of June 5, up 2.6% from a year earlier, a survey from consultancy Mysteel showed.

Steel consumption, however, showed signs of weakening as high summer temperatures hindered construction.

Other steelmaking ingredients, including coking coal NYMEX:ACT1! and coke (DCJcv1) were up 3.91% and 1.19%, respectively.

Most steel benchmarks on the Shanghai Futures Exchange advanced. Rebar ticked 0.85% higher, hot-rolled coil added 0.81%, wire rod (SWRcv1) climbed 0.88% while stainless steel nudged down 0.08%.

($1 = 7.1834 Chinese yuan)

data.title: Matsa Resources Says Pre-Mining Activity at Western Australia Gold Mine Underway; Shares Jump 5%
data.description:
Matsa Resources said pre-mining activities at its Devon Pit gold mine in Western Australia are underway, with the starter pit completed, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

First ore is expected to be mined in June, the filing said. The mining fleet is being mobilized, and the commissioning of the dewatering infrastructure at the mine is completed, with all water to be discharged onto Lake Carey.

The company also conducted grade control drilling with the initial focus being on the starter pit and the northern part of the main lode.

The ore will be stockpiled at the site, and ore haulage to FMR Investments' Greenfields Mill will start in July, where the first parcel of 50,000 tonnes of ore is expected to be processed in mid-late September.

The firm's shares jumped nearly 5% in recent trading on Friday.

data.title: Koonenberry Gold Says Drilling at New South Wales Gold Project Returned Significant Mineralization, Shares Fall 27%
data.description:
Koonenberry Gold said a diamond drilling hole at the Enmore gold project's Sunnyside prospect in New South Wales returned significant gold mineralization over extensive widths, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The results included an intercept of 150 meters at 0.71 grams per tonne grade of gold from 230 meters. Gold mineralization remains open up-dip and at depth as well as along strike.

Results from another drill hole, which contained visible gold, are expected in mid to late June.

The firm's shares fell 27% in recent trading on Friday.

data.title: Elon Musk Net Worth Shrinks by $27 Billion
data.description:
By WSJ Staff

Elon Musk suffered one of his worst financial days, losing about $27 billion from his net worth. The Tesla and SpaceX boss is still the world's richest man with an estimated net worth of about $388 billion at Thursday's market close, according to the Forbes Real Time Billionaires List.

He remains well above No. 2 Mark Zuckerberg ($236 billion) and President Trump, whose net worth is estimated at $5.4 billion, putting him at No. 689 on the Forbes ranking.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Japan 10-Year Yield Falls to Near 3-Week Low
data.description:
Japan’s 10-year government bond yield fell to around 1.45% on Friday, reaching its lowest level in nearly three weeks after demand for a 30-year bond auction turned out better than anticipated.

Earlier in the week, a solid 10-year bond auction also helped ease pressure across the broader bond market.

The pullback in yields comes after a sharp rise in late May, when the 10-year yield approached levels not seen since 2008 and the 30-year yield hit record highs.

The surge was driven by growing investor concerns over the sustainability of Japan's fiscal position amid the Bank of Japan’s gradual withdrawal from the bond market.

In response, reports suggest the Ministry of Finance is considering reducing issuance of super-long-term bonds to alleviate upward pressure on yields.

Meanwhile, BOJ Governor Kazuo Ueda has indicated that the central bank will likely continue tapering its JGB purchases in the next fiscal year, reinforcing expectations of a slow but steady policy normalization path.

data.title: ASX Biggest Gainers
data.description:
Here are the 10 ASX-listed companies with the biggest gains on Friday.

data.title: China Central Bank to Conduct 1-Trilion-Yuan Reverse Repo Operations
data.description:
The People's Bank of China will conduct a 1-trillion-yuan reverse repurchase operation on Friday.

The reverse repo operation will have a three-month term or 91 days, and could be conducted through fixed-quantity, interest-rate bidding, the central bank said.

data.title: Vietnam Tourist Arrivals Up 10.5% YoY in May
data.description:
International arrivals to Vietnam increased by 10.5% year-on-year to 1.53 million in May 2025, following a 6.3% growth in the previous month.

Visitors from Asia rose by 8.4%, mainly led by China (14.1%), Malaysia (17.9%), Japan (15.9%) and Singapore (18.6%).

Additionally, arrivals from Europe climbed by 35.1%, particularly from Russia (171.2%), the UK (12.2%), and Sweden (2.9%); while, tourists from Australia grew by 8.3%, while those from Africa jumped by 3.8%.

Meanwhile, arrivals from Africa dropped by 8.5%.

For the first five months of the year, tourist arrivals rose by 21.3% to 9.20 million.

data.description:
Stocks Slip, Tesla Tanks as Trump-Musk Feud Rages

Rising jobless claims and a sharp decline in imports raised the specter of slowing economic growth.

----

Fed's Kugler: Higher Inflation Bigger Risk Than Weak Employment

The Fed governor supports keeping interest rates at current levels if the risk of higher inflation remains.

----

Treasury to Double Down on Currency Manipulation, Issues Warning for China

----

Canadian Companies No Longer Fear 'Catastrophic' Fallout From Tariffs, Central Bank Says

Silver prices just hit a 13-year high. More gains could be on the way.

Broadcom Sales Climb on Booming AI Demand

The maker of semiconductors and software posted higher profit and sales, thanks to demand for AI networking as hyperscalers continue to invest.

data.title: In surprise victory, UK's Labour wins Scottish by-election after bitter contest
data.description:
Key points:
  • Labour defies predictions to win by-election
  • Unexpected victory eases pressure on PM Starmer
  • Nigel Farage's Reform finishes in third place

British Prime Minister Keir Starmer's Labour Party won a surprise victory in a fiercely fought by-election for the Scottish parliament on Friday after one of the most bitter election campaigns in the country's recent history.

In an election when race became a major issue, Labour won the seat from the Scottish National Party in Hamilton, Larkhall and Stonehouse following the death of a former government minister.

The result provides some relief for Starmer, whose party has suffered a steep fall in support since it won a landslide in a British general election last year after it raised taxes, cut welfare benefits and got into a row over the use of donations.

Voters rallied around Labour in the closely watched contest after Reform UK, led by Nigel Farage, repeatedly referred to the ethnicity of the Scottish Labour leader Anas Sarwar, who is of Pakistani heritage, drawing criticism from the other parties.

The Labour candidate Davy Russell won 8,559 votes in the election for the Scottish parliament, which has devolved powers over issues like health and education. The SNP won 7,957 votes, and Reform finished third with 7,088 votes.

The betting odds had predicted a comfortable victory for the SNP with Labour pushed into third place behind Reform.

Russell told his supporters that his victory had "sent a message to Farage and his mob, the poison of Reform isn't us, it isn't Scotland and we don't want your division here".

But a recent surge in support for Reform suggests the party is making inroads in Scotland as well as in England, a year before the Scottish parliament elections are held next year.

The support for Reform in Scotland is particularly surprising because the party's brand of low tax, anti-immigration, anti-EU politics, has often been more associated with English nationalism.

Farage visited Scotland on Monday, days after a row over an online video put out by his party that falsely claimed Sarwar had said he would "prioritise" the Pakistani community.

Labour branded the advert "blatantly racist" and the SNP leader John Swinney urged voters to reject Reform's "gutter politics". Farage responded by accusing Sarwar of introducing sectarianism into Scottish politics.

data.title: Infinity Lithium to Start Drilling at Victoria Project
data.description:
Infinity Lithium will start drill-testing the CST gold-silver prospect within the Cobungra project in Victoria in July, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

The prospect will undergo a first-pass drilling campaign to identify additional priority targets and areas of geological interest, the filing said.

The company is awaiting the mobilization of a diamond drill rig, expected to arrive within four to five weeks, per the filing.

data.title: Japan's Nikkei rises on demand for futures, a weaker yen
data.description:
Japan's Nikkei share average rose on Friday, as a weaker yen underpinned sentiment, while demand for the index futures ahead of the fixing of special quotation prices lifted the benchmark cash index.

The Nikkei was up 0.47% to 37,730.67 by the midday break and is set to fall 1% for the week.

The broader Topix rose 0.56% to 2,771.81 and is poised to fall 1.6%.

The Nikkei received technical support ahead of the June 13 fixing of special quotation prices, used to set values on index options and futures, said Seiichi Suzuki, chief equity market analyst at Tokai Tokyo Intelligence Laboratory.

"Foreigners, who shorted the Nikkei futures, bought them back ahead of the fixing day, which also lifted the Nikkei index," he said.

The Nikkei average futures (JNIc1) rose 0.59%.

A weaker yen was also a support for local equities, said Shigetoshi Kamada, general manager at the research department at Tachibana Securities.

The yen was last traded down 0.23% at 143.885 per dollar.

Chip-related heavyweights Tokyo Electron and Advantest rose 1.28% and 0.98%, respectively.

Automakers rose, with Honda Motor and Nissan Motor gaining 1.19% and 1.38%, respectively, while Toyota Motor inched up 0.17%.

Flea market app operator Mercari jumped 5.94% to become the top percentage gainer on the Nikkei, while Oriental Land , the operator of Tokyo Disneyland, rose 2.95%.

"When caution persists over the upside of the Nikkei, investors, particularly individuals, tend to look at smaller and domestic-led companies," said Tachibana Securities Kamada.

Panasonic Holdings , a supplier of batteries for Tesla , lost 3.58% after Tesla shares plunged 14.3%

Shares of ispace were untraded after the moon exploration company said that its uncrewed moon lander likely crashed onto the moon's surface during its lunar touchdown attempt. The stock reached to a limit-low of 744 yen.

data.title: Botala Energy Completes Drilling at Botswana Coal Bed Methane Project
data.description:
Botala Energy completed drilling at the Serowe-3.4A well, the second well in its Project Pitse pilot cluster within the Serowe coal bed methane project in Botswana, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

Testing showed gas desorption rates ranging from 3.2 grams per cubic centimeter (cc/g) to 4.21 cc/g in the Serowe coal seam, confirming high gas content, the filing said.

The well is currently being completed, and connectivity with the Serowe-3.1 well is being tested, per the filing.

data.title: Chengdu Expressway Awards 780 Million Yuan Contract for Expansion Project
data.description:
Chengdu Expressway said its wholly owned subsidiary Chengdu Chengwenqiong Expressway has signed a 779.8 million yuan civil construction contract for the TJ6 section of the Chengwenqiong Expressway Expansion Project, according to a Thursday Hong Kong bourse filing.

Xinjiang Beixin Road & Bridge Group won the open tender to upgrade a 9.09-km stretch to a two-way, eight-lane expressway with a 100 km/h design speed. Works include two medium bridges, 11 small bridges, and interchanges, according to the filing.

The project is expected to take 30 months to complete and will be funded through internal resources and bank loans.

The expansion project forms part of Sichuan's 2022-2035 expressway development plan and is aimed at easing congestion in the western Chengdu area. The total investment for the overall project is estimated at 12.65 billion yuan.

Xinjiang Beixin Road & Bridge Group is a state-backed infrastructure firm specializing in road and bridge construction.

data.title: Financial Times - June 6
data.description:
The following are the top stories in the Financial Times. Reuters has not verified these stories and does not vouch for their accuracy.

Headlines

- HSBC's Mark Tucker to become chair of insurer AIA

- UK bans bonuses at Thames Water and 5 other utilities

- UK makes new concessions to private equity on tax crackdown

- Reform UK chair Zia Yusuf resigns from party

Overview

- HSBC's high-profile Chairman Mark Tucker will exit the bank to take on a new role as chairman of AIA Group , the pan-Asian insurance firm where he once served as CEO.

- Thames Water and five other water companies have been banned from paying bonuses to their bosses for failing to tackle pollution, in Britain's latest effort to overhaul the industry's poor environmental record.

- Britain unveiled new concessions to private equity firms over its crackdown on a tax break enjoyed by industry executives on Thursday, following warnings the reform would damage British competitiveness.

- Zia Yusuf, the chairman of Britain's right-wing Reform UK party, resigned abruptly on Thursday following a row with its newest lawmaker, becoming the latest senior figure to exit the populist party.

data.title: Poll: Republicans Side With Trump Over Musk, While Democrats Want Neither
data.description:
By Tim Hanrahan

A quick online poll from YouGov conducted Thursday evening found Americans siding with President Trump over Elon Musk, though many said they would stay out of the dispute completely. Asked whether they would back Trump, Musk or neither, 28% said Trump, while 8% said Musk. Fifty-two percent said neither.

Republicans strongly sided with Trump, 71% to 6%, with 12% saying neither. Democrats mostly favored neither, but of those who made a choice, 11% backed Musk to just 4% for Trump. The survey was conducted online among 3,812 U.S. adults, and was weighted by age, gender, party and other measures. YouGov said the margin of error for the overall sample was about 2%.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Greentown China's May Self-Investment Sales Hit 18.6 Billion Yuan
data.description:
Greentown China recorded sales of 18.6 billion yuan from its self-investment projects in May, according to a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

The company sold 2,895 units in May, with a total sales area of 420,000 square meters and an average selling price of 44,102 yuan per square meter, the filing said.

From January to May, self-investment contracted sales reached 65.4 billion yuan, of which 44.4 billion yuan was attributable to the group.

Sales from its project management business totaled 7.8 billion yuan in May, with a sales area of 620,000 square meters. Cumulative sales for this segment reached 31.1 billion yuan over five months, covering 2.33 million square meters.

Total sales for the group, including self-investment and project management, reached 96.5 billion yuan for the five-month period.

data.title: Intermestic's All-Store Sales Up 7.7% in May
data.description:
Intermestic's all-store sales rose 7.7% year on year in May, while same-store sales increased 3.6%, according to a Thursday filing with the Tokyo bourse.

The number of stores remained unchanged at 314 from March.

The eyewear retailer attributed the better performance to popular fashion-related collaboration products, TV commercials and UV-related products.

data.title: Market Chatter: KHNP to Open Czech Republic Office for Nuclear Project
data.description:
Korea Electric Power subsidiary Korea Hydro & Nuclear Power, or KHNP, plans to establish an on-site construction office at the Dukovany nuclear power plant in the Czech Republic to speed up its newly secured reactor project, Yonhap News reported Thursday.

The decision comes after KHNP and Czech state-run utility Elektrárna Dukovany II signed the final agreement to construct two 1,063-megawatt reactors at the Dukovany nuclear power plant, following a court decision to lift an injunction that had delayed the deal.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Rupee weakness set to linger in lead up to RBI verdict, US jobs data
data.description:
The Indian rupee’s downward bias looks set to persist at Friday’s open, with traders awaiting the Reserve Bank of India’s policy decision and the U.S. jobs report due later in the day.

The RBI outcome is expected to provide cues on the domestic rate outlook, while the latter could shape expectations for U.S. interest rates.

The 1-month non-deliverable forward indicated an open in the 85.86-85.90 range, versus 85.79 in the previous session. The rupee inched up 0.1% against the U.S. dollar on Thursday, finding support at the 86 level.

While the rupee found slight relief on Thursday, the "overall direction of travel" remains on the downside, a currency trader at a Mumbai-based bank said.

"With the RBI policy due shortly, it should be quiet after the opening move higher (on dollar/rupee)," he said.

The trader expects the dollar/rupee to be supported at 85.60-85.70 and pegs resistance at 86-86.10, reckoning that the range will hold up after the two catalysts.

The RBI is widely expected to cut interest rates by 25 basis points, marking its third straight reduction amid a backdrop of benign inflation. India's largest lender by assets, State Bank of India, is more dovish and is calling for a 50 bps rate cut.

A 50-bps "will not be received well" by the rupee and will lead to a sizeable up move on the dollar/rupee at least initially, the currency trader said.

Inflation forecast revision and comments by the RBI head will be watched for cues on what comes next for interest rates.

Meanwhile, the U.S. non-farm payrolls report, due later in the day, will attract attention amid signs that President Donald Trump’s tariffs are weighing on the economy.

Investors are currently pricing in two rate cuts by the Federal Reserve this year. A weaker-than-expected jobs report could increase expectations for further cuts, putting additional pressure on the already struggling dollar.

"The US labour market is so essential in underpinning market confidence that we must be prepared and reactive if we were to see an outlier outcome," Chris Weston, head research at broker Pepperstone, said.

KEY INDICATORS:

** One-month non-deliverable rupee (INRNDFOR=) forward at 85.98; onshore one-month forward premium at 10.5 paisa

** Dollar index at 98.81

** Brent crude futures down 0.2% at $65.2 per barrel

** Ten-year U.S. note yield at 4.40%

** As per NSDL data, foreign investors bought a net $186.8mln worth of Indian shares on June 4

** NSDL data shows foreign investors sold a net $130.7mln worth of Indian bonds on June 4

data.title: Nanshan Aluminium Investing 1.9 Billion Yuan in Indonesia Chemical Plant
data.description:
Shandong Nanshan Aluminium will invest 1.87 billion yuan in a caustic soda and epichlorohydrin plant in Indonesia.

The investment will be made via a joint venture, PT Bintan Fine Chem Indonesia, according to a Friday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Construction for the project, with an annual production of 200,000 tons of caustic soda and 165,000 tons of epichlorohydrin, will take about 18 months.

Shares of the aluminum company rose less than 1% higher in recent trade.

data.title: Kelun-Biotech: Upsized Deal Marks Largest Follow-On Offering in Biopharma Industry in Hong Kong in Year >6990.HK
